rationale: The citing paper uses Tuli, Bharadwaj, and Kohli (2010) in its theory development, not just in a background list. Specifically, it cites that study as evidence that stronger customer relationships reduce switching and stabilize revenues, which supports the proposed cash-flow volatility channel for marketing capability. This role feeds directly into the paper's conceptual logic for why marketing capability can buffer the effect of negative news on stock returns.
relation_description: The citing paper relies on Tuli et al. (2010) as prior empirical support that relationship strength can stabilize sales/cash flows. That evidence serves as part of the conceptual foundation for the citing paper's marketing capability mechanism.
